title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Flatbush, Brooklyn | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Flatbush, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat extraction from homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call for fast service!
intro: Flatbush residents dealing with bats in attics, chimneys, or walls need immediate professional help to safely remove these protected animals. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Flatbush, from the Victorian homes near Prospect Park South to the apartment buildings along Flatbush Avenue. Our licensed technicians use humane methods to remove bats and seal entry points, protecting your family and property.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has over 15 years of experience serving Flatbush homeowners with specialized bat removal expertise that complies with all NYC wildlife protection laws. Our team uses proven exclusion techniques and provides warranties on our work, ensuring bats won't return to your Flatbush property.
localContext: Flatbush's mix of century-old Victorian homes, pre-war apartment buildings, and Dutch Colonial residences creates numerous opportunities for bat roosting in attics, eaves, and chimneys. The tree-lined streets near neighborhoods like Ditmas Park and proximity to Prospect Park make this area particularly attractive to bat populations seeking shelter in residential structures.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Flatbush?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Flatbush typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in NYC?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected wildlife in New York, so removal must be done humanely by licensed professionals. We use one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, and we never harm or relocate bats. Our methods comply fully with all NYC and state wildlife regulations.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Flatbush home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark staining near entry points, accumulation of guano (droppings) in attics or near walls, and seeing bats emerge from your roof or chimney at sunset. You may also notice a musty ammonia-like odor from bat waste.

faqs.question: How long does bat exclusion take in Flatbush homes?
faqs.answer: The exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks from initial installation to final sealing. We install one-way exclusion devices and monitor to ensure all bats have left before permanently sealing entry points. The timeline may be longer during maternity season (May-August) when baby bats are present, as we wait until young bats can fly.
